channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Roof appears to leak ahead of Michigan-Washington college football national title game
item.description: The roof at NRG Stadium appeared to be leaking ahead of the CFP national title game between the Michigan Wolverines and Washington Huskies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Packers' Jaire Alexander hilariously bombs reporter's live broadcast where she doesn't recognize him
item.description: Green Bay Packers cornerback Jaire Alexander hilariously bombed a reporter's live broadcast hit outside Lambeau Field, but she didn't know he played for the green and yellow.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Four Giants assistant coaches fired after 6-11 season
item.description: EAST RUTHERFORD, N.J. (AP) — New York Giants special teams coordinator Thomas McGaughey, offensive line coach Bobby Johnson, outside linebackers coach Drew Wilkins and defensive assistant Kevin Wilkins were fired Monday, a day after the team finished a 6-11 season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 49ers assistant GM Peters reportedly targeted by other NFL teams for promotion
item.description: Peters, who has interviewed for GM jobs before, is just the latest 49ers front-office face to be coveted by opposing teams.
item.dc:creator: Michael Nowels, The Mercury News

item.title: Sharks update: What’s missing from William Eklund’s game right now?
item.description: San Jose Sharks forward William Eklund is on an extended goal drought going into Tuesday's game with the Toronto Maple Leafs
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: Iconic Brazilian soccer player, coach Mario Zagallo dies at 92
item.description: He was the first person to win World Cup championships as a player and as a manager. He also was the last living member of Brazil's starting team from 1958, the country's first championship squad.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Franz Beckenbauer, who won the World Cup both as player and coach for Germany, has died at 78
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Franz Beckenbauer, who won the World Cup both as player and coach and became one of Germany’s most beloved personalities with his easygoing charm, has died. He was 78.
item.dc:creator: CIARÁN FAHEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man United beats 3rd-tier Wigan 2-0 to ease through to 4th round of FA Cup
item.description: WIGAN, England (AP) — Manchester United avoided becoming the highest-profile casualty in the third round of the FA Cup by easing past third-tier Wigan with a 2-0 win on Monday.
item.dc:creator: AP, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saquon Barkley 'numb' to potential Giants franchise tag this offseason: 'Gonna let my agents handle that'
item.description: Saquon Barkley knows that he heads into an offseason that could be just like it was a year ago, as the New York Giants can hand him another franchise tag.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's why you may not be able to watch the Dolphins-Chiefs NFL wild-card game
item.description: The Dolphins-Chiefs wild-card game will be the first NFL playoff contest – but for a few exceptions – exclusively streamed on Peacock, an NBC subscription service.
item.dc:creator: Nexstar Media Wire, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Franz Beckenbauer was a graceful and visionary 'libero' who changed the face of soccer
item.description: By taking a step back, Franz Beckenbauer put himself a step ahead. The “libero” — taken from the Italian word for “free” and describing a player who had a covering role behind a defensive line — was not an entirely new concept to soccer by the late 1960s and early 1970s.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: For Saints coach Dennis Allen, players' decision to score late vs. Atlanta was no mutiny
item.description: Saints coach Dennis Allen is taking a nuanced view of his players’ decision to go against coaches' wishes by orchestrating a 1-yard touchdown run by Jamaal Williams in the final minute of a season-ending 48-17 victory over Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vote now: Bay Area News Group girls athlete of the week
item.description: Make your choice for the top performance among girls high school athletes from the first week of January.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lefkow, Correspondent, The Mercury News

item.title: Wander Franco released from jail in Dominican Republic
item.description: Video above: Wander Franco arrived in court on Friday TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) -- Wander Franco, who is under investigation for allegedly having a sexual relationship with a 14-year-old, was released Monday from a jail in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NFL Playoff Guide: How to watch the games, bracket format and betting favorites
item.description: The Buffalo Bills and Dallas Cowboys are riding high after big Week 18 wins capped their regular seasons.
item.dc:creator: David Brandt, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Houston, the lone unbeaten team in college hoops, jumps Kansas to No. 2 in AP Top 25 behind Purdue
item.description: Purdue held its place atop the AP Top 25 on Monday following its win over Illinois in a top-10 showdown, while Houston jumped Kansas into second place as the last unbeaten team in Division I men's college basketball. The Boilermakers, who beat the No.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rams' Puka Nacua sets rookie records for yards and catches
item.description: Puka Nacua couldn't have scripted a much better ending to his first regular season in the NFL than what played out on Sunday. A touchdown catch. Two NFL records. A celebration with mom. And a win for the Los Angeles Rams.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW AP Pro Football Writer, KTLA 5

item.title: CFP championship is a preview of expanded Big Ten; Washington, 3 others join conference in 2024
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — The College Football Playoff championship is quite the stage for Washington and Michigan to get reacquainted with each other. The teams play for the national title tonight. But starting next season, they’ll compete against each other for Big Ten titles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Justin Fields faces offseason of uncertainty as Bears debate whether to take a QB with No. 1 overall pick
item.description: GREEN BAY, Wis. (AP) — Chicago Bears quarterback Justin Fields has been through all this before. That helps explain why he refuses to spend the offseason dwelling on his uncertain future as the Bears debate whether to keep him as their starter or select his replacement with the No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bulldog Insider preview: Kalen DeBoer, Washington prep for CFP national championship game
item.description: HOUSTON (KSEE) - There is one game left in the college football season: the CFP National Championship. On Monday night in Houston, Texas, Washington plays Michigan. Most of Washington's coaching staff was in Fresno as recently as 2021. "You want to empower your staff.
item.dc:creator: Angelique Martinez, KSEE, CBS

item.title: Bulldog Insider feature: Allen Huddleston leading revival at Merced College
item.description: MERCED, Calif. (KSEE) - Allen Huddleston averaged nearly 32 points per game as a senior at Merced High School, and he had a solid college career as a player in two years at both Pacific and at Fresno State.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bemis, KSEE, CBS

item.title: LeBron James scores 25 points, Lakers hold off Clippers 106-103 to snap 4-game losing streak
item.description: LeBron James had 25 points, eight rebounds and seven assists, Taurean Prince hit a tiebreaking 3-pointer with 1:17 to play and the Los Angeles Lakers snapped a four-game losing streak with a 106-103 victory over the Los Angeles Clippers on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Puka Nacua breaks two NFL rookie records
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - With his third catch Sunday against the San Francisco 49ers, Los Angeles Rams and former Orem High and BYU star wide receiver Puka Nacua broke a 63-year-old record for the most receiving yards by a rookie in NFL history.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: 'Playoffs are always the best': Bucs fans celebrate NFC South win
item.description: The Bucs are traveling back to Tampa after securing the win on the road against the Carolina Panthers on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jets snap 15-game skid vs Patriots with 17-3 win in Belichick's possible finale as New England coach
item.description: FOXBOROUGH, Mass. (AP) — Breece Hall rushed for 174 yards and a late touchdown and the New York Jets snapped a 15-game losing streak against the Patriots with a 17-3 win Sunday in what could have been Bill Belichick’s final game as New England's coach.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Texans win AFC South title, to host Browns in rematch game on KPRC 2 on Saturday at 3:30 p.m.
item.description: Sunday's loss by the Jacksonville Jaguars puts the Texans on top of their division for the first time since the 2019 season.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Wilson, KPRC 2

item.title: Blackwell helps the Blackhawks beat the Flames 4-3 in first game without Bedard
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Colin Blackwell had two goals and an assist and the Chicago Blackhawks won their first game since Connor Bedard was sidelined by a broken jaw, topping the Calgary Flames 4-3 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Alex Smith elected to College Football Hall of Fame
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Quarterback Alex Smith has been elected to the College Football Hall Fame, becoming the first Utah player ever to receive that honor. Legendary Utes coach Ike Armstrong was elected in 1957.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah State shuts down #13 Colordado State, 77-72
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - It was a special night at The Spectrum, and the Utah State fans felt like celebrating. Great Osobor scored 20 points and had 14 rebounds and Mason Falslev added 18 points to lift the Aggies over No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: BYU drops Big 12 opener to Cincinnati, 71-60
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - In its much-anticipated Big 12 Conference opener, the BYU basketball team turned in its worst offensive performance of the season, and fell at home to Cincinnati, 71-60. BYU had been averaging 90 points per game, and winning by an average of 28 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Draymond Green reinstated by NBA after suspension
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- After being handed an indefinite suspension last month, Draymond Green has been reinstated by the NBA, the league announced Saturday afternoon. Green's suspension ended up being 12 games.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Carlsbad hoops makes San Diego history at Holiday Classic
item.description: Carlsbad High School head basketball coach Clark Allard and his squad are riding high after becoming the fifth San Diego area team to win the national division title at the 33rd Torrey Pines Holiday Classic.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Warriors G Chris Paul breaks hand, needs surgery
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors will be without Chris Paul for the foreseeable future. The veteran guard suffered a fractured left hand in the Warriors' win over the Detroit Pistons on Friday and will need surgery, the team confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Athlete of the Week: Memorial Tigers Bradley Manso
item.description: EAST RUTHERFORD, N.J. (PIX11) -- From 113 players representing every team in the New Jersey Super Conference to eight finalists to one young man of the year.
item.dc:creator: Perry Sook, PIX 11

item.title: Physical locations lagging as North Carolina sports betting looms
item.description: The window for sports betting to become legal in North Carolina starts on Monday, Jan. 8, though officials said that will not be happening.
item.dc:creator: Derek Dellinger, NewsNation

item.title: Reliever Woo-Suk Go agrees to $4.5 million, 2-year deal with San Diego Padres
item.description: South Korean reliever Woo-Suk Go and the San Diego Padres agreed Wednesday to a $4.5 million, two-year contract that includes a 2026 mutual option plus bonus and escalator provisions that could make the deal worth $9.4 million over three seasons if he closes regularly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Worthy to NFL, Norris Jr. to showcase
item.description: Tuesday's Central Valley sports news included a Central High alum declaring for the NFL Draft, and an outgoing Fresno State defensive star from Sanger getting an opportunity to impress scouts at a college all-star showcase.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bemis, KSEE, CBS
